name: County Cork Irish Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 Large leek, white part only thinly sliced
Parsley, bay leaves, peppercorns, thyme, rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the lamb chops with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in a wide saucepan that can accommodate all chops in a single layer.
3. Brown the chops on both sides.
4. Remove any melted fat from the pan.
5. Add enough water to cover the chops, then bring to a boil.
6. Add parsley, bay leaf, peppercorns, thyme, and rosemary (wrapped in cheesecloth) to the pot.
7. Lower the heat and let simmer.
8. Peel the potatoes and cut them into bite-sized rounds.
9. Chop any potato trimmings into small pieces.
10. Add the potatoes, potato trimmings, cabbage, chopped onions, well-rinsed leek, white onions, and celery to the chops and liquid.
11. Simmer for 20 minutes, then add the peas.
12. If needed, add a little more water during cooking.
13. Continue simmering for an additional 10 minutes or until the potatoes are tender.
14. Adjust seasoning if necessary.
15. Garnish with parsley and serve.
